title: Binary search returns wrong index
issue: binary_search in src/search.py finds the value, but returns the wrong position when it does. Looks like the success branch is off by one.

title: find_max returns minimum instead of maximum
issue: find_max in src/stats.py is backwards right now. It keeps the smallest value instead of the largest one.

title: Fibonacci returns None for n >= 2
issue: fibonacci(0) and fibonacci(1) are fine. Anything above that comes back as None, so the recursive path is probably missing the actual return.

title: format_greeting raises KeyError
issue: format_greeting blows up with a KeyError when I pass a normal user dict. The lookup key in src/greetings.py does not match the data the tests use.

title: average() crashes on empty list
issue: average([]) currently dies with ZeroDivisionError. I want a ValueError instead, with a useful message, so it needs an early guard.

title: top_k returns the wrong scores
issue: top_k is returning the bottom scores, not the top ones. Check the sort direction and what slice the function keeps.

title: count_occurrences is case-sensitive when it should not be
issue: count_occurrences('apple', ['apple', 'Apple', 'APPLE']) should be 3, but it comes back as 1. The comparison in src/collections_utils.py needs to ignore case on both sides.
note: easy bug, but the loop sometimes patches only one side of the comparison and then gets stuck

title: is_valid_email rejects .info and other longer TLDs
issue: The regex in src/validator.py is too strict. user@example.info should pass, but the current pattern only accepts very short TLDs.

title: sample_variance is computing population variance
issue: sample_variance is dividing by N instead of N - 1. The code in src/statistics_utils.py is using the population formula where the tests expect sample variance.
note: worth checking the expected values before touching the math, just in case

title: reverse_words does nothing
issue: reverse_words('hello world') still returns 'hello world'. The split is there, but the function never actually reverses the pieces before joining them back up.
